content: We can reframe the ICM curiosity mechanism for autonomous agents around an episodic-semantic node learning framework to resolve the longstanding ambiguity of how episodic and semantic memory interact: the ICM’s intrinsic reward for novel state discovery learns to dynamically weight shared semantic node connections between past exploratory episodes, turning scattered episodic memory and stable semantic memory representations. This combined framework generates a testable hypothesis: agents fine-tuning their node embedding space via ICM curiosity will develop more generalizable exploration than standard ICM, because semantic node connections are reinforced only when they prove useful across multiple distinct exploratory episodes.
